title: silene dioica subsp. glutinosa
intro: Silene dioica subsp. glutinosa, also known as red campion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of red campion include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Red campion can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and digestive issues.
